title: Astracantha consentanea
intro: Astracantha consentanea, also known as Centaury, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in promoting digestion, relieving gastrointestinal issues, and aiding in the treatment of coughs and respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for various health concerns. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha consentanea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Astracantha consentanea is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused in herbal teas, and is also used in combination with other herbs in various herbal preparations, such as cough syrups and digestive tonics.
taxonomy: Astracantha consentanea is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and is part of the genus Astracantha. Within the Astracantha genus, it falls under the tribe Genisteae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships. Astracantha consentanea shares similarities with other Astracantha species in its classification.
